goal: Convert the remaining hook and factory top-level compatibility wrappers into module aliases instead of `*` re-export wrappers.
constraints: Preserve public import paths and runtime behavior
Do not move implementations again in this tranche
behavioral_changes: The remaining top-level hook and factory compatibility modules now alias the moved implementation modules directly.
Legacy and namespaced import paths now share module identity instead of only symbol re-exports.
Public APIs and file locations remain unchanged.
verification_targets: PYTHONPATH=src python -m pytest tests/test_evaluate.py tests/test_suggest.py tests/test_packets.py tests/test_watchtower.py tests/test_graduation.py tests/test_loop_controller.py -q
follow_up: The remaining work is mainly package-boundary decision and documentation, not more compatibility wrapper conversion.
Keep the alias modules in place until any future package split has a migration path.
